faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Is SMART on FHIR free to use?
faq.a1: Yes, SMART on FHIR is an open standard that anyone can use for free. Both the underlying FHIR specification and SMART application framework are open source.
faq.q2: How long does it take for a hospital to implement SMART on FHIR?
faq.a2: It depends on existing systems. Basic FHIR interfaces can typically be completed in 3-6 months, while full SMART platform integration may take 6-12 months. We provide phased implementation guides to help you plan.
faq.q3: What specific support does the Initiative provide?
faq.a3: We provide technical consulting, adoption assessment tools, developer training, policy recommendations, and cross-border collaboration networks. All foundational resources are free.
faq.q4: How is data security and privacy protected?
faq.a4: SMART on FHIR has a built-in OAuth2 security framework supporting fine-grained data access control. We comply with each country's data protection regulations (China's Data Security Law, Japan's APPI, Australia's Privacy Act, etc.).
faq.q5: How can I contribute to the community?
faq.a5: You can contribute to open-source projects on GitHub, join online forum discussions, attend events, or apply to become a regional ambassador. See the Get Involved page for details.
